name: Figma Context MCP
tagline: Give your coding agent direct access to your Figma design data
seoTitle: Figma Context MCP — Figma design data for AI coding agents
metaDescription: Figma Context MCP (Framelink) is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that gives Cursor and other AI coding tools access to your Figma files to build UIs.
overview: Figma Context MCP, also known as Framelink MCP for Figma, is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that connects AI-powered coding tools to your Figma files. When a tool like Cursor can read the actual design data instead of a screenshot, it is far better at turning a design into working code in one pass.

Instead of passing the full Figma API response to the model, the server first simplifies and translates it so only the most relevant layout and styling information is included. Sending less but more focused context helps the model stay accurate and keeps its responses on topic.
features: Exposes Figma file, frame, and group data to AI coding agents over the Model Context Protocol
Simplifies and translates raw Figma API responses into compact layout and styling context
Designed to work with Cursor and other MCP-compatible AI clients and code editors
Reduces the amount of context sent to the model to improve accuracy and relevance
Configured through a standard MCP server entry, run on demand with npx
Uses a Figma personal access token, supplied as a CLI flag or the FIGMA_API_KEY environment variable

gettingStarted.intro: Most code editors and AI clients use a configuration file to manage MCP servers. You first need a Figma personal access token, then you add the figma-developer-mcp server to that config file.

steps.heading: Create a Figma access token
steps.body: You need a Figma API access token to use this server. Create a personal access token in your Figma account settings, following Figma's official instructions, and keep it ready to paste into your config.

steps.heading: Add the server on macOS or Linux
steps.body: Add the figma-developer-mcp server to your MCP configuration file, replacing YOUR-KEY with your Figma access token.

steps.heading: Add the server on Windows
steps.body: On Windows, wrap the same command with cmd /c. As an alternative to the CLI flag, you can set FIGMA_API_KEY and PORT in the env field instead.

steps.heading: Use it from your editor
steps.body: Open your IDE's chat (for example agent mode in Cursor), paste a link to a Figma file, frame, or group, and ask the agent to do something with it, such as implement the design. The agent fetches the relevant Figma metadata and uses it to write your code.

useCases: Implementing a Figma design as front-end code in one pass from inside Cursor or another AI coding tool
Letting an AI agent read accurate layout and styling details from a frame instead of guessing from a screenshot
Building a UI in any framework by pointing your coding agent at a specific Figma file, frame, or group
